title: Attention in Image Captioning: Show, Attend & Tell
concept: Attention mechanisms allow decoders to focus on specific spatial regions of an image when generating each word of a caption.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main role of the LSTM in image captioning?

options.text: To generate the caption word-by-word based on the visual feature vector.
To extract spatial filters from raw pixels.
To crop the objects out of the image.

exercises.explain: Correct! The LSTM functions as the decoder, translating visual embeddings into sequential text output.

cards.title: Dynamic focus
cards.body: When you look at a photo and write a caption, you don't look at the whole picture when writing every single word. When you write the word 'ball', your eyes are focused on the ball. When you write the word 'grass', your eyes shift to the ground. Attention mechanisms give neural networks this ability to 'look' at specific pixels dynamic.

cards.title: What is visual attention?
cards.body: Visual Attention is a mechanism that allows the text decoder to focus on different spatial regions of the CNN's feature map for each predicted word. The model calculates an attention heatmap over the image grid, weighting the visual features before generating the next word.

cards.title: The reading flashlight
cards.body: Imagine reading a picture book in a dark room with a small flashlight. When you read and write about the castle, you point the flashlight at the castle. When you write about the dragon, you swing the flashlight to the top corner. Attention acts as this flashlight, highlighting only the relevant pixels for each step.

cards.title: 'Show, Attend and Tell'
cards.body: In the famous 'Show, Attend and Tell' paper, researchers visualized the attention grid during inference. When the model output the word 'girl', the attention weights spiked on the pixel region containing the child. When it predicted 'tennis', focus shifted to the racket.

cards.title: Not all pixels are equal
cards.body: A common myth is that decoders use the same visual feature vector for the entire sentence. Without attention, the model tries to remember the whole image at once, which often causes it to miss small objects. Attention dynamically updates the visual input for every word.

cards.title: No-Attention vs. Attention Models
cards.body: How models use image features during word generation:

left.text:
Same global feature vector for all words
Lacks spatial alignment
Struggles with small details

right.text:
Attention weights update per word
Highlights active visual zones
High detail, interpretable weights

exercises.prompt: What does a visual attention mechanism do in an image captioning model?

options.text: It weights different spatial regions of the image feature map for each generated word
It resizes the image to make it fit a square crop
It deletes background pixels to save GPU memory
It categorizes the overall sentiment of the text

exercises.explain: Attention calculates weights over visual regions, focusing the decoder on relevant parts for each predicted word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In attention-based captioning, the visual feature representation updates for every word the model generates.
exercises.explain: True. The attention weights are re-calculated at every step, creating a dynamic visual context vector for each word.

exercises.sentence: Visual attention models generate a dynamic spatial ___ over the image coordinates.
exercises.explain: The attention grid acts as a spatial heatmap, indicating where the model is looking.

exercises.prompt: Which description represents visual attention in action during generation of the word 'bird'?

options.text: The model's attention weights spike on the pixels containing the bird, ignoring the background sky
The model outputs a coordinate box around the whole sky
The model classifies the image as 'wildlife'

exercises.explain: Focusing weight specifically on the bird's pixels is the signature behavior of visual attention.

exercises.prompt: You are debugging a model that outputs the caption: 'A girl playing tennis.' The image shows a girl holding a tennis racket in her room, but there is no court. Profiling shows the model is confusing the racket for a full tennis match. How can you fix this?

options.text: Visualize the attention heatmaps to verify if the model is focusing on the racket while predicting 'tennis', and retrain with more diverse indoor scenes.
Disable the attention mechanism to force the model to look at nothing.
Convert the image to black-and-white to remove colors.
Increase the learning rate of the LSTM decoder.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Visualizing attention maps lets you audit what the model is looking at. Finding that it associates a racket with a tennis court suggests context bias, which is fixed by diversifying the training dataset. Outstanding! Next: modern Transformer captioning.
